title: The Double-Edged Sword of Workplace Surveillance
subtitle: 职场监控：效率与隐私的两难

paragraphs.en: Many oppose workplace surveillance because of the inherent dehumanizing effect it has and the relentless pressure it brings. But it's on the rise around the world as firms look to become more efficient by squeezing more productivity from their workers. More than half of companies with over $750 million in annual revenue used non-traditional monitoring techniques on staff last year. Monitoring employee performance gives firms the ability to assess how their staff are performing and interacting, which can be good for both firm and employees.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人反对职场监控，因为它具有固有的非人化效应并带来无情的压力。但随着企业试图通过压榨员工生产力来提高效率，它在全球范围内呈上升趋势。去年，年收入超过7.5亿美元的公司中有一半以上对员工使用了非传统监控技术。监控员工绩效使公司能够评估员工的表现和互动方式，这对公司和员工都有好处。

paragraphs.en: A growing number of analytics companies offer this service. They gather 'data exhaust' left by employees' email and instant messaging apps, and use name badges equipped with radio-frequency identification devices and microphones. These can check how much time you spend talking, your volume and tone of voice, even if you do not dominate conversations. While this may sound intrusive, exponents argue that it can also protect employees against bullying and sexual harassment.
paragraphs.zh: 越来越多的分析公司提供这项服务。他们收集员工电子邮件和即时通讯应用留下的“数据废气”，并使用配备射频识别设备和麦克风的名牌。这些设备可以检查你说话的时间、音量和语调，甚至你是否主导对话。虽然这听起来具有侵入性，但支持者认为它也可以保护员工免受欺凌和性骚扰。

paragraphs.en: Some data analysis can produce unexpected results. For example, it was found that people who sat at 12-person lunch tables tended to interact more, share ideas and outperform those who regularly sat at four-person tables — a fact that would have gone undetected without such analysis. Over the last few years a Stockholm co-working space called Epicenter has gone further and holds popular 'chipping parties', where people can have microchips implanted in their hands. They can use the implants to access electronically-controlled doors or monitor how typing speed correlates with heart rate.
paragraphs.zh: 某些数据分析能产生意想不到的结果。例如，研究发现坐在12人餐桌旁的人比常坐4人桌的人互动更多、分享更多想法且表现更好——若没有此类分析，这一事实可能永远不会被发现。过去几年里，斯德哥尔摩一家名为Epicenter的联合办公空间走得更远，举办颇受欢迎的“芯片派对”，人们可以在手中植入微芯片。他们可以用植入物开启电子门禁，或监测打字速度与心率的相关性。

paragraphs.en: Implanted chips may seem extreme, but it is a relatively small step from ID cards and biometrics to such devices. As long as such schemes are voluntary, there will probably be a growing number of convenience-oriented uses. But if implanted chips are used to reduce slack time or rest breaks, that could prove detrimental. And if surveillance tools take away autonomy, that's when they prove most unpopular. A lot depends on how such monitoring initiatives are communicated, which could prevent possible revolts.
paragraphs.zh: 植入芯片或许显得极端，但从身份证和生物识别到此类设备只是相对较小的一步。只要此类计划是自愿的，以便利为导向的用途可能会越来越多。但如果植入芯片被用来减少闲暇时间或休息时间，则可能有害。而如果监控工具剥夺了自主权，那正是它们最不受欢迎的时候。很大程度上取决于如何传达此类监控举措，这可能防止潜在的反抗。

paragraphs.en: If bosses don't communicate effectively, employees assume the worst. But if they're open about the information they're collecting and what they're doing with it, research suggests 46 percent of employees are generally okay with it. Although many such schemes use anonymised data and participation is voluntary, many staffers remain sceptical and fear an erosion of their civil liberties. So workplace surveillance could be empowering or it could become an unpopular tool of oppression that proves counterproductive.
paragraphs.zh: 如果老板沟通不力，员工就会往最坏处想。但如果他们对收集的信息及其用途保持透明，研究表明46%的员工大体上可以接受。尽管许多此类计划使用匿名数据且参与是自愿的，许多员工仍持怀疑态度，担心公民自由受到侵蚀。因此，职场监控既可能赋权员工，也可能成为适得其反的压迫工具。
